title: Commercial office, Type V-B, egress corridor below the 44-inch minimum
notes: Tests IBC 1020.3: the narrowest labeled corridor (38 in) is below the 44 in general minimum. The engine sees the width but not the corridor's served occupant load (44 in only binds at OL >= 50; 36 in is allowed below), so the deterministic rule emits needs_review for human confirmation rather than a hard violation. Area/stories/exits are clean so only the corridor rule fires.
plan_text:
CODE ANALYSIS A-0.1
Occupancy Group: B
Construction Type: V-B
Stories: 1
Building height: 16 ft
Per-story area: 6,000 sf
Fully sprinklered: yes
Design occupant load: 70
IECC: Title 24 Part 6
Electrical: 200 A main breaker
NFPA 13 sprinkler system. NFPA 72 fire alarm.
Corridor width: 44 in (north). Corridor width: 38 in (south at column). Corridor width: 52 in (lobby).
EXIT 1 (north). EXIT 2 (south). EXIT DOOR 36 in each.
WC 4 LAV 4. CALGreen low-flow fixtures. Construction waste management plan.

ground_truth.rationale: 6,000 sf sprinklered single-story B/V-B is within the Table 506.2 allowance

Narrowest corridor is 38 in, below the 44 in IBC 1020.3 general minimum. The engine can't confirm the corridor's served occupant load (44 in binds only at OL >= 50), so it flags needs_review rather than asserting a violation.
